name: KrillApp.Executor.Calculation
title: Calculation
description: Evaluates a mathematical formula that references source DataPoint values. Node IDs in the formula are enclosed in brackets (e.g., [node-id]) and replaced with current snapshot values at execution time. Supports arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /, %, ^), mathematical functions (sin, cos, tan, sqrt, abs, ln), and parenthesized expressions. The computed result is written to a target DataPoint.
shortDescription: Computes a mathematical formula from source DataPoint values and writes the result to a target DataPoint.
llmPurpose: Evaluates a mathematical formula referencing source DataPoint values and writes the computed result to a target DataPoint.
llmBehavior: resolves node ID references in formula to current values
llmOutputs: computed numeric result written to target DataPoint
llmTypicalUseCases: derive a calculated value from multiple sensors
llmPromptHints: compute the average of these sensors
llmCreationHints: Create a Calculation when the user needs a derived value from one or more DataPoints using a mathematical formula. Requires a formula string, source DataPoints, and a target DataPoint.

llmConnectionHints.role: transform node that computes a formula and writes the result to a target

llmExamples: Calculate the sum of these two sensor readings.
Convert this temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit with [sensor1] * 9/5 + 32.
Compute the difference between these two data points.
Create a weighted average of three sensors.
